item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: timer expired, edits made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

The only link I see here is the possibility for a link to Dr. Wondertainment. I propose linking this to the official hub.

Editing this:

Description: Manufacturer's packaging identifies SCP-1553 as “Dr. Wondertainment®'s Shadow Paint Play-Set!”

To this:

Description: Manufacturer's packaging identifies SCP-1553 as “Dr. Wondertainment®'s Shadow Paint Play-Set!”

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very clever. I personally am curious if you can communicate if you make a human shadow and whether the shadow person is hostile or friendly depending on who draws it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm dissapointed that there wasnt a test log of drawing a human and trying to communicate with it, but still +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This could maybe do with a couple more experiment logs, where you prove some of the things you talk about (humanoid shadows etc.), but I still liked it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm actually working on designing a diagram or two to make it easier to see. This was my first successful SCP ever… and it shows when you compare it to my more recent work.

Weirdest thing: it's actually quite simple, but trying to describe it in clinical language is really complicated. We'll see if I can do those diagrams well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is horrendously confusing. No vote for now while I read it again and try to get it to make sense.

Okay… you can paint things with the paint as though they were the shadows of actual objects, and then interact with them, and if you paint a living thing, the shadow will behave like that living thing. You can erase shadows with the sponge, and if it was cast by a real object, the object kinda disappears.

I feel it could be much better explained, but it's a cool idea, and reasonably well explored.

One question: if you can interact with the shadows as if they were real things, what does that look like? When the first test subject grasped the hammer, did the shaft run over the outside of his fist? Or was the part of it that would be obscured by his hand were it a real hammer obscured? If the shadow cat stood on your shoulder, you'd feel it there, would you not? And your shadow would look like you had a real cat on your shoulder? So what would happen if you moved away?

Argh it makes my head hurt

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The mechanics are a bit confusing, particualrly the phantom object and it's position/motion in space. But I'm not sure if that is something that can be fixed in the writing, it's a pretty mechanically confusing concept. One of those things that would be easier to demonstrate than it is to explain. Too bad that's not an IRL option, this would be fascinating stuff to play experiment with.

I thought the seed idea had potential when I first saw it, and I'm glad to see that potential so well realized.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Real classic Wondertainment stuff here. I especially love "Ani-Magi-Nation".

I will admit to being confused by the bulleted list, specifically by the term "phantom object", which doesn't appear prior to it. Does this paint create an object in real space in a position such that it would be casting the painted shadow? Or is that term meant to denote the shadow itself?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd say he has a fair claim... ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love how Wondertainment tries to claim a trademark on the word "real".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dig breach Protocol 012-Trundholm. Experiment 37, which I think is the juiciest one, is not as clear as it could be. To me, it reads as though the same parts that lost coherency and required cleanup also remained suspended in mid-air at the same time. I don't quite follow how that went down. Oh, maybe it was all the other parts that required cleanup? I guess that makes sense, but it could stand to be rephrased. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huh…I don't see it.

Can you be specific?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Some grammar needs to be fixed here and there, but the Scip was generally nice.

Downvote for now til' it gets fixed

Edit: All good now, Upvotes for you! :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So… the wya you make it sound is if you apply it to a surface, it make a random shadow object. But then you say you can make specific shadow objects? Maybe if you made it so what ever shadow falls across the painted surface…. dunno.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
White-Lab 02 is to be kept under constant remote video surveillance.

It sounds like the lab is supposed to be kept lit 24 hours a day. If that's right, that should be explicitly noted, and the containment procedure should also say something about a replacement schedule for the lights.

item.title: Criticisms and Semantics
item.content:encoded:
I personally think you could just stick it in any old box and call it a day, but it's not bad. It just highly passes a Locked Box Test.

The comma in the middle there is unnecessary.

Also, do these shadows exist solely in 2d space? As in, they only exist on walls, floors, etc? They need surfaces on which to exist, if I'm not mistaken?

Moving on…

Document 1553-C impresses me, good job staying in line with the tone of Wondertainment advertisements.

The concept is good, but your language and tone in describing it is…kind of hard to follow. The description tells us what it is, yes, but parts of it are kind of obfuscated such that its hard to be sure about the object itself.

Also, you mention Incident 1553-1 but have no Incident attached. Is this because it's coming later or was it an intentional decision to leave it out?

Also, your picture mentions White Lab-01, but the containment procedures mention White Lab-02.

All in all, it's pretty good, might need a midge of work, but manages to be highly clinical despite being a Wondertainment SCP. Upvoting for concept, clinical tone, and especially the Wondertainment advertisement.
